member.name: T:Ampere.AmpFile.FileUtils

The line number to replace from

member.name: M:Ampere.AmpFile.FileUtils.ReplaceInLines(System.IO.FileInfo,System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.Collections.Generic.KeyValuePair{System.String,System.String},System.Int32})
member.summary: Replaces all instances of a specific value from a file with another replacement value from a specified line. This overload facilitates the replacement through a Dictionary where the key is an instance of and the value is an int. This allows for unique replacements to occur in more than one line.
member.param: The FileInfo instance to write the value to
A Dictionary of replacement values and line numbers

A Dictionary of replacement values and line number

member.summary: Selects elements from a weighted distribution using the Efraimidis–Spirakis algorithm. Each element is associated with a weight that determines its probability of selection. Runs in O(n log k).

member.name: M:Ampere.Base.Shuffler.Derange``1(System.ReadOnlySpan{``0})
member.summary: Produces a random derangement of the given array using Sattolo's algorithm. A derangement is a permutation where no element appears in its original position. Returns a new array; the original is not modified.

member.returns: A new array representing a random derangement of the source

member.name: M:Ampere.Base.Shuffler.RandomDouble
member.summary: Generates a uniformly distributed random double in (0, 1) exclusive using cryptographic randomness. Uses 53 bits of entropy to fill the full mantissa of a double-precision float.

The method also allows for inserting more than one element into the array at one time given that they are specified.

The amount of elements to insert into the array
Optionally, the values to insert into the empty indices of the new array

member.name: M:Ampere.Enumerable.EnumerableUtils.ToString``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Prints a string representation of an enumerable. There are 4 supported lengths for the fmtExp. The default length is 0 and the default behavior depends on the type of the enumerable. If the type is primitive (based on the System.IsPrimitive property) including decimal and string, then it prints the enumerable with a space as a separator between each element. A fmtExp of length 1 specifies a character to separate each element. A fmtExp of length 2 specifies two characters to mark the left and right outer bounds of the enumerable. A fmtExp of length 3 specifies a character for the left outer bound, a separator character, and a character for the right outer bound. The "/0+" expression disables separators.
member.param: The defined expression to be optionally used
Determines whether the spacing between each element should be the same
member.returns: The string representation of the enumerable
member.exception: If arr is null
If the formatting expression length is neither 0 nor 3

member.name: M:Ampere.Enumerable.InnerContainsProgram`1.CheckContains
member.summary: Checks whether each array specified in otherArrays is completely contained in the base array. Each value in each array must be contained in the base array for this method to return true, otherwise, false is returned and the ViolatedEnumerable property is set with the enumerable that violated the condition. This function runs a LINQ intersection between each enumerable.
member.returns: True if each enumerable is contained in the base and false otherwise.

member.name: M:Ampere.Str.StringBuilderUtils.ReplaceInterval(System.Text.StringBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary:
Returns this instance of the StringBuilder in which a specific occurrence of a specified string in the current instance is replaced another specified string on a integer defined interval. This allows certain occurrences/intervals of text up to a predefined stop count. This is an overload of
Given the following string, "very very very very very very very", StringBuilder.ReplaceInterval("very", "happy", 2, sb.Length, StringComparison.CurrentCulture) will replace every second "very" with the word "happy" until the very end of the string. The result would be: "very happy very happy very happy very"
Given the following string, "very very very very very very very", StringBuilder.ReplaceInterval("very", "happy", 2, 3) will replace every second "very" with the word "happy" until the third occurrence of "very". The result would be: "very happy very very very very very"
member.param: The string to replace an occurrence of
The interval the replacement should follow - read as, "Replace every nth occurrence of "
member.returns: This StringBuilder instance

member.name: M:Ampere.Str.StringBuilderUtils.ReplaceInterval(System.Text.StringBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Boolean)

Given the following string, "very very very very very very very", StringBuilder.ReplaceInterval("very", "happy", 2, 3) will replace every second "very" with the word "happy" until the third occurrence of "very". The result would be: "very happy very very very very very"
member.param: The string to replace an occurrence of
The interval the replacement should follow - read as, "Replace every nth occurrence of "
The condition to satisfy before replacing the value
member.returns: This StringBuilder instance

member.name: M:Ampere.Str.StringBuilderUtils.ReplaceInterval(System.Text.StringBuilder,System.String,System.String,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.StringComparison,System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Returns this instance of the StringBuilder in which a specific occurrence of a specified string in the current instance is replaced another specified string on a integer defined interval. This allows certain occurrences/intervals of text up to a predefined stop count.
Given the following string, "very very very very very very very", StringBuilder.ReplaceInterval("very", "happy", 2, sb.Length, StringComparison.CurrentCulture) will replace every second "very" with the word "happy" until the very end of the string. The result would be: "very happy very happy very happy very"
Given the following string, "very very very very very very very", StringBuilder.ReplaceInterval("very", "happy", 2, 3) will replace every second "very" with the word "happy" until the third occurrence of "very". The result would be: "very happy very very very very very"
member.param: The string to replace an occurrence of
The interval the replacement should follow - read as, "Replace every nth occurrence of "
The number of found instances of to search before stopping
One of the enumeration values that determines how is searched within this instance
The condition to satisfy before replacing the value
member.returns: This StringBuilder instance

member.param: The specific StringBuilder action to carry out for the StringBuilder
member.returns: The StringBuilder instance
